skill.name: file-organizer
skill.description: Organizes local files and folders by analyzing directory contents, finding duplicates, proposing folder structures, and guiding approved cleanup actions.
skill.summary: Plan and execute safer cleanup for messy local folders.
skill.author: DYAI2025

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command and blocker patterns, but most blocker hits are false positives from prose, Markdown examples, or non-security duplicate detection. The real risk is that the skill encourages broad local filesystem inspection and approved move, rename, and deletion workflows, so it should publish with a clear filesystem warning.

medium_findings.title: Broad Local Filesystem Modification Workflow
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to organize files by creating folders, moving files, renaming files, and handling duplicate deletion after approval. This is legitimate for a file organizer, but it can cause data loss if the user approves an incorrect plan or if paths are misunderstood.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The workflow explicitly covers file moves, renames, folder creation, and duplicate deletion decisions. The same text also requires confirmation before deletion, which reduces but does not remove the risk.

medium_findings.title: Shell Commands With User-Selected Paths
medium_findings.description: The skill provides examples using ls, find, du, mkdir, and mv against user-selected directories. These are expected for the task, but unsafe path handling or overly broad scopes could expose private file metadata or alter the wrong directory.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The listed commands are visible in Markdown examples and are semantically aligned with file organization. Risk depends on runtime path choices, so confidence is high for the pattern but not for malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are Duplicate-Detection False Positives
low_findings.description: The md5 example is used to compare file contents for duplicate detection, not for passwords, signatures, encryption, or authentication. Other weak-crypto locations from static analysis appear to be prose or formatting matches with no security-sensitive cryptographic use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only concrete hash command appears in the duplicate-finding section. No evidence found of security-sensitive cryptographic decisions or secret protection.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Findings Are Benign File-Organization Context
low_findings.description: The scanner reported reconnaissance-like terms, but the referenced content describes messy Downloads folders and naming practices. No evidence found of network scanning, host discovery, or collection for exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are ordinary user-facing file organization guidance. They do not instruct network probing or system enumeration beyond local folder review.

dangerous_patterns.title: Filesystem Enumeration Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill suggests commands that list directory contents, inspect file types, measure folder sizes, and count extensions. These commands can reveal sensitive local filenames and metadata when run on broad folders.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are directly present and enumerate user-selected directories. They are expected for this skill, but they still carry privacy risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: File Move And Rename Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes mkdir and mv examples for approved cleanup. These commands are legitimate but can permanently disrupt user workflows if applied to the wrong paths.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The examples show direct filesystem mutation commands. The surrounding instructions require approval and logging, which lowers severity.

dangerous_patterns.title: Duplicate Deletion Decision Flow
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill guides agents to recommend duplicate removal and ask before deleting. The confirmation requirement is good, but duplicate detection can misclassify important versions if context is incomplete.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The duplicate-removal flow is clearly documented. Risk is moderate because deletion is conditional on user confirmation rather than automatic.

content.value_statement: Messy folders make files hard to find and increase duplicate storage. This skill analyzes local directories, proposes clear structures, and guides approved cleanup actions.
actual_capabilities: Reviews directory contents and summarizes file types, sizes, and structure issues.
Finds likely duplicate files by hash, name, and file size comparisons.
Proposes folder structures for Downloads, Documents, photos, projects, and archives.
Creates an approval-based plan before moving, renaming, archiving, or deleting files.
Provides maintenance guidance for weekly, monthly, quarterly, and yearly cleanup.
limitations: It requires access to the local folders that the user chooses to inspect.
It cannot guarantee that duplicate-looking files are safe to delete without user review.
It depends on shell commands that may differ between macOS, Linux, and Windows environments.
It should not organize sensitive folders without a narrow scope and explicit approval.

use_cases.title: Clean a crowded Downloads folder
use_cases.description: Group documents, images, installers, archives, and temporary files into a clearer structure.

use_cases.title: Prepare project archives
use_cases.description: Separate active projects from old work and create an archive layout by year or client.

use_cases.title: Reduce duplicate storage
use_cases.description: Find duplicate files in shared folders and prepare a review list before cleanup.

prompt_templates.title: Beginner Folder Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Downloads folder and summarize what is inside. Do not move, rename, or delete anything yet.

prompt_templates.title: Duplicate Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Find likely duplicate files in this folder. Show each group with paths, sizes, dates, and a recommendation. Ask before deleting anything.

prompt_templates.title: Project Archive Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze my Projects folder and propose a structure that separates active work, archived work, templates, and experiments. Wait for approval before changes.

prompt_templates.title: Approved Cleanup Execution
prompt_templates.prompt: Using the approved plan, create the folders, move the agreed files, log each change, and stop if any filename conflict appears.

output_examples.input: Review my Downloads folder before making changes.
output: Summary of file types, largest folders, and recent files.
A proposed folder structure for documents, media, installers, archives, and items needing review.
A list of questions before any move or deletion occurs.

output_examples.input: Find duplicates in my Documents folder.
output: Grouped duplicate candidates with file paths, sizes, and modification dates.
A keep-or-delete recommendation for each group.
A confirmation prompt before removing any file.
Current structure issues and naming inconsistencies.
A proposed Active, Archive, Templates, and Learning layout.
A step-by-step change plan that waits for approval.

best_practices: Start with one folder and request a review-only summary before approving changes.
Back up important files before running move, rename, archive, or delete actions.
Review duplicate recommendations manually because similar files can represent different versions.
anti_patterns: Do not ask it to reorganize an entire home folder in one pass.
Do not approve deletions unless each path and reason is clear.
Do not run cleanup on sensitive or shared folders without a narrow scope.

faq.question: Can this skill delete files?
faq.answer: It can guide deletion of duplicates or trash files, but the instructions require confirmation before deleting anything.

faq.question: Does it work on all operating systems?
faq.answer: The examples use common shell commands. Some commands may need changes on Windows or restricted environments.

faq.question: Can it organize photos by date?
faq.answer: Yes. It can propose year and month folders using EXIF data or file modification dates when available.

faq.question: Is it safe for sensitive files?
faq.answer: Use a narrow folder scope and review every plan. Directory scans can expose filenames, sizes, and dates.

faq.question: How does it find duplicates?
faq.answer: It suggests comparing file hashes, names, sizes, paths, and modification dates before recommending what to keep.

faq.question: Can it undo changes?
faq.answer: It recommends logging all moves for possible reversal, but undo quality depends on the detail of that log.
